overview: The Village of Weston Plan Commission approved a site plan for the new Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive, and adopted a new salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection areas. Both items passed unanimously with all members present voting in favor.

agenda.item: Call to order and roll call
Site plan review for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive
Creation of salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
April 2026 staff approved certified survey maps and site plans
Planning and development department monthly project update report

discussions.item: Site plan review for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility
discussions.body: Staff presented the site plan for the new police department and municipal court at 3600 Community Center Drive. The project meets all B2 zoning district requirements. Police Chief Jeremy explained challenges with a gas pipeline requiring the building to be rotated and positioned on the higher part of the site to reduce fill costs. The building has capacity for 50-60 officers (current staffing is 40 officers and 4 clerical). An alternate storage shed was discussed, with commissioners suggesting it be placed further north to accommodate future building expansion. Motion was made to recommend approval to the Board of Trustees subject to staff conditions 2 through 5. Motion passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
discussions.body: Staff proposed a program with two annual inspections per facility - a warm weather inspection focusing on structural integrity and site drainage, and a winter operational inspection focusing on material handling and containment. A standardized inspection worksheet similar to DOT requirements was developed. Staff noted one existing facility near Sternberg that has never gone through the wellhead protection conditional use process and is closest to the village well. Commission agreed staff should reach out to that facility. Motion to approve the program with reporting back to both Plan Commission and Public Works Committee passed unanimously.
Motion to approve the April 13, 2026 meeting minutes as presented passed unanimously with no discussion.

discussions.item: April 2026 staff approved certified survey maps and site plans
Planning and development department monthly update
discussions.body: Staff announced the first meeting of the joint Plan Commission/Village Board steering committee for the comprehensive plan update is scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 5:00 p.m. Staff will send calendar invites.

publicComment: Jim Pensel of 5002 Arrow Street, Weston, spoke via remote connection. He requested the commission urge staff to waive excessive landscaping requirements for the police department project similar to accommodations made for Greenheck Turner, to save taxpayer dollars. He also asked that the commission request an annual report on salt shed inspections to ensure follow-through, noting some zoning complaints are years old.
actionItems: Site plan for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court at 3600 Community Center Drive recommended for approval to Board of Trustees subject to staff conditions
Salt storage shed inspection program adopted for wellhead protection district with twice-annual inspections
Staff to report back to Plan Commission and Public Works Committee on salt shed inspection findings next year
Staff to reach out to existing salt storage facility near Sternberg that has not gone through wellhead protection conditional use process
Joint Plan Commission/Village Board steering committee meeting for comprehensive plan scheduled for June 1st at 5:00 p.m.
Electric vehicle charging station regulations to be discussed at future meeting in fall
Next regular meeting scheduled for Monday, June 8, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.
title: Plan Commission
